record.field: GitLab CE is a complete, self-hosted DevOps platform that unifies Git repository management, code review, CI/CD, and project planning in a single application.

GitLab CE is a complete, self-hosted DevOps platform that unifies Git repository management, code review, CI/CD, and project planning in a single application.

The free, MIT-licensed Community Edition shares the same code base as GitLab Enterprise Edition, giving teams the full software-development lifecycle on their own infrastructure without per-seat licensing. It covers source control, issue tracking, merge requests, automated pipelines, a container and package registry, and wikis, so the entire workflow lives in one tool. Self-hosting keeps source code and build artifacts fully under your control, which suits privacy-sensitive and air-gapped environments.

Key features
  • Git repository hosting with branch protection, code review, and merge requests
  • Built-in CI/CD pipelines defined as code, with runners and an integrated container registry
  • Issue tracking, boards, milestones, and epics for project and product management
  • Integrated wiki, code snippets, and a built-in web IDE
  • Fine-grained roles, groups, and access controls for teams and organizations
  • Distributable via official Docker images and scalable across multiple servers
Best for

Engineering teams and organizations that want a single self-managed platform for source control, CI/CD, and collaboration, and that prefer keeping their code and pipelines on their own infrastructure for compliance, privacy, or cost reasons.

services: gitlab: image: gitlab/gitlab-ce:latest restart: unless-stopped hostname: gitlab.example.com environment: GITLAB_OMNIBUS_CONFIG: 'external_url ''http://{{ instance.full_domain }}''
gitlab_rails[''gitlab_ssh_host''] = ''{{ instance.full_domain }}''
gitlab_rails[''gitlab_shell_ssh_port''] = 2224
gitlab_rails[''db_adapter''] = ''postgresql''
gitlab_rails[''db_host''] = ''postgresql''
gitlab_rails[''db_port''] = ''5432''
gitlab_rails[''db_database''] = ''{{ POSTGRES_DB }}''
gitlab_rails[''db_username''] = ''{{ POSTGRES_USER }}''
gitlab_rails[''db_password''] = ''${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}''
# Redis config for external TCP connection
gitlab_rails[''redis_url''] = ''redis://redis:6379/0''
gitlab_rails[''redis_host''] = ''redis''
gitlab_rails[''redis_port''] = 6379
gitlab_rails[''redis_socket''] = nil
gitlab_rails[''gitlab_email_enabled''] = false
gitlab_rails[''gitlab_default_can_create_group''] = true
gitlab_rails[''gitlab_username_changing_enabled''] = false
unicorn[''worker_processes''] = 2
unicorn[''worker_timeout''] = 60
postgresql[''enable''] = false
redis[''enable''] = false
nginx[''enable''] = true
nginx[''listen_port''] = 80
nginx[''listen_https''] = false
prometheus_monitoring[''enable''] = false
' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' volumes: - ./gitlab_config:/etc/gitlab - ./gitlab_logs:/var/log/gitlab - ./gitlab_data:/var/opt/gitlab depends_on: - postgresql - redis container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network postgresql: image: postgres:16-alpine restart: unless-stopped environment: POSTGRES_DB: {{ POSTGRES_DB }} POSTGRES_USER: {{ POSTGRES_USER }} POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} volumes: - ./postgresql_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgresql' networks: - internal redis: image: redis:7-alpine restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./redis_data:/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_redis' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgresql sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgresql for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgresql sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgresql sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgresql sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
